text.title: Agentic RAG — Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure from input brief to signed-off output code.

list.item: Input: Domain task description.
Action: Define Pydantic GraphState (question, documents, generation, hop_count, route_decision).
Output: state.py with GraphState class.
Decision gate: If state grows > 10 fields, split sub-states.
Input: Vector store + (optional) web search tool.
Action: Build router node + vectorstore node + web search node.
Output: Graph nodes connected with router-driven edges.
Decision gate: If web search is not in scope, route web→reject.
Input: Relevance + groundedness prompts.
Action: Wire relevance grader (post-retrieve) + groundedness grader (post-generate).
Output: Two grader nodes with binary or 0-1 score output.
Decision gate: If graders are noisy, train a small classifier instead of LLM grading.
Input: Hop counter in state.
Action: Set max_hops=3; on overflow return degraded answer + uncertainty flag.
Output: Graph compiles with the cap enforced.
Decision gate: If queries routinely exceed cap, retrieval quality is poor — revisit chunking.
Input: Eval suite with multi-hop queries.
Action: Run eval, record (hop_count, hallucination_rate, latency_p95), iterate.
Output: Per-route metric dashboard.
Decision gate: If hallucination_rate > 5%, return to step 3 (graders).
